title: Classification of Sets by Cardinality
description: A flowchart that categorizes sets into finite, countably infinite, and uncountable sets, with key examples for each category.
relationships: Finite Sets have a specific number of elements
Countably Infinite Sets can be put into a one-to-one correspondence with ℕ
Uncountable Sets are infinite but cannot be listed, like ℝ
ℕ, ℤ, and ℚ are all examples of Countably Infinite Sets
ℝ is an example of an Uncountable Set
